up.units: mM (total consumed over batch, S2/42 h)
up.method: enzymatic assay (R-Biopharm); cumulative consumed = L_WPM - L_final

up.units: mM (total consumed over batch, S2/42 h)

up.method: enzymatic; glucose (0.05 g/l) present but not consumed
enzymatic; citrate (0.04 g/l) present but not consumed

sec.method: enzymatic; no ethanol produced under anaerobic growth

growth_records.cite: Ricciardi, Annamaria et al. Effect of Respiratory Growth on the Metabolite Production and Stress Robustness of Lactobacillus casei N87 Cultivated in Cheese Whey Permeate Medium. Frontiers in Microbiology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.00851. [PMC6491770]
growth_records.snippet: Cond AN E1 mu_max 0.65 +/- 0.00 ... AN E2 0.31 X_max 2.20; Lactose 110.1 +/- 1.60 galactose 18.4 lactate(DL) 331.4 acetate 22.4 ethanol 0.0 acetoin 0.2 (S2)
growth_records.notes: Batch bioreactor, anaerobic. mu_max reported (Table 1, DMFit primary dynamic model): E1 (primary growth)=0.65/h, E2 (diauxic second growth)=0.31/h (R2 0.995-0.999). doubling_time_h from mu E1: ln2/0.65=0.693/0.65=1.066 h. X_max E1=0.21 g/l, E2=2.20 g/l. Uptake/secretion in Table 2 are CUMULATIVE net concentrations (fermented minus un-inoculated WPM), not per-gDW-h rates; reported here as consumed/produced mM at end of fermentation (S2, 42 h). Yxs = X_max(E2) / total sugar consumed = 2.20 g/l / (110.1 mM*342.30 g/mol=37.69 g/l lactose + 18.4 mM*180.16=3.31 g/l galactose = 41.0 g/l) = 0.054 g/g. Lactate/hexose = 331.4 mM lactate / (2*110.1 + 18.4 = 238.6 mM hexose-equiv) = 1.39 mol/mol (homolactic max 2). WP measured 38.01+/-0.25 g/l lactose, 3.82+/-0.60 g/l galactose.

up.units: mM (total consumed over batch, S2/42 h)
mM (total consumed over batch, S2/42 h)

up.method: resazurin discoloration assay in air-saturated buffer + 5.5 mmol/l glucose; time to discoloration indicates O2 uptake, no rate quantified

growth_records.cite: Ricciardi, Annamaria et al. Effect of Respiratory Growth on the Metabolite Production and Stress Robustness of Lactobacillus casei N87 Cultivated in Cheese Whey Permeate Medium. Frontiers in Microbiology (2019). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2019.00851. [PMC6491770]
growth_records.snippet: RS E1 mu_max 0.61 +/- 0.02 ... RS E2 0.29 X_max 2.64; Lactose 110.8 galactose 18.4 lactate 299.8 acetate 92.5 diacetyl 2.3 acetoin 12.0 (S2). Respiration significantly increased acetate (up to 4x in S2), diacetyl and acetoin.
growth_records.notes: Batch bioreactor, respiratory (aerobic + hemin/menaquinone). mu_max (Table 1): E1=0.61/h, E2=0.29/h; respiration did NOT significantly change mu vs anaerobic. doubling_time_h=ln2/0.61=1.136 h. X_max E1=0.20, E2=2.64 g/l. Uptake/secretion are CUMULATIVE net mM at end (S2, 42 h) from Table 2, not per-gDW-h. Yxs = 2.64 / (110.8 mM*342.30=37.93 g/l lactose + 18.4 mM*180.16=3.31 g/l gal = 41.24 g/l) = 0.064 g/g. Lactate/hexose = 299.8 / (2*110.8+18.4=240.0 mM) = 1.25 mol/mol. Diacetyl 2.3 mM*86.09=198 mg/l (~text '200 mg/l'); acetoin 12.0 mM*88.11=1057 mg/l (~text '1.02 g/l'). Respiration boosted acetate 22.4->92.5 mM and rerouted pyruvate to acetoin/diacetyl (pox/als/oad up-regulated by RT-PCR).

up.method: Glucose consumption measured over time in co-culture (Fig. S3C) but no numeric rate reported in text

sec.method: L-lactate production measured in co-culture (Fig. S3), higher than mono-culture; no numeric rate in text
D-lactate produced (from co-cultured E. coli) after ~10 h (Fig. S3); no numeric rate in text

growth_records.cite: Mizuno, Kouhei et al. Adjacent-possible ecological niche: growth of Lactobacillus species co-cultured with Escherichia coli in a synthetic minimal medium. Scientific Reports (2017). DOI:10.1038/s41598-017-12894-3. [PMC5643319]
growth_records.snippet: co-culture with E. coli supported growth in this medium, with co-cultured L. casei reaching a density of 10^8 CFU ml-1 after 6 h ... During log-phase growth in the co-culture, the specific growth rate was 0.396 h-1, approximately 67% that o
growth_records.notes: L. casei is a prokaryote; grows in M9 only when co-cultured with E. coli. µ=0.396 h-1 reported (log phase, 3-6 h). Doubling time td=ln2/µ=0.693/0.396=1.75 h (calculated). Glucose consumption and L-/D-lactate production reported (Fig. S3) but only as figures, no numeric rates -> rate=null, is_experimental true. Fig. 6 reports additional L. casei co-culture µ (3-6 h) at varying initial L. casei inoculum (10^4,10^5,10^6,10^7 CFU/mL; E. coli fixed at 10^6): values listed as 0.311, 0.370, 0.177, 0.102, 0.302 h-1 (NOTE: paper lists 5 rates for 4 inoculum levels - internal inconsistency, not mapped here). Also note inoculum-ratio discrepancy: Methods state pre-cultures 'inoculated at 1:100', while Results state the main co-culture ratio was 10:1 (L. casei:E. coli). Static, 37 C.

sec.method: L. casei is a primary L-lactate producer (homofermentative); no numeric rate reported for MRS mono-culture

growth_records.cite: Mizuno, Kouhei et al. Adjacent-possible ecological niche: growth of Lactobacillus species co-cultured with Escherichia coli in a synthetic minimal medium. Scientific Reports (2017). DOI:10.1038/s41598-017-12894-3. [PMC5643319]
growth_records.snippet: During log-phase growth in the co-culture, the specific growth rate was 0.396 h-1, approximately 67% that of the mono-culture in MRS (µ ≒ 0.6 h-1).
growth_records.notes: L. casei NBRC 3831 mono-culture in rich MRS medium; µ reported as approximately 0.6 h-1 (symbol ≒, i.e. 'nearly equal'). Doubling time td=ln2/0.6=0.693/0.6=1.16 h (calculated). Value given as reference for the co-culture (0.396 h-1 = ~67% of 0.6). L-lactate secretion flagged is_experimental=false because it is inferred from species phenotype, not a reported measurement for this specific MRS culture. Static, 37 C, 24 h.

up.method: Glucose consumed in M9 mono-culture despite no CFU increase (Fig. S3A); no numeric rate reported

sec.method: L-lactate produced in M9 mono-culture (Fig. S3B) despite lack of proliferation; no numeric rate reported

growth_records.cite: Mizuno, Kouhei et al. Adjacent-possible ecological niche: growth of Lactobacillus species co-cultured with Escherichia coli in a synthetic minimal medium. Scientific Reports (2017). DOI:10.1038/s41598-017-12894-3. [PMC5643319]
growth_records.snippet: During mono-culture in M9, L. casei consumed glucose and produced l-lactate (despite the lack of increase in CFU number) ... L. casei is metabolically active even under conditions that are insufficient for cell proliferation.
growth_records.notes: Distinct condition: L. casei alone in M9 (no growth support). No proliferation (CFU ~flat) so growth_rate_per_h set to 0 and doubling_time null. Retained because glucose uptake and L-lactate secretion were experimentally measured (Fig. S3), though no numeric rates are given in the text. Demonstrates metabolic activity without growth.
